key_insight: SILAGE is a variance-reduced nonconvex optimizer for nested finite sums that removes costly full-dataset gradient refreshes while using only O(n) memory.
engineering_relevance: It is well suited for large-scale, partitioned training settings such as data silos, out-of-core learning, and stratified datasets where memory and communication are bottlenecks.
method: Exploits the double-sum structure to update using at most one local group gradient per iteration.
Avoids periodic global full-gradient computations over all nm samples.
Uses only O(n) memory instead of storing per-sample control variates.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a tight convergence analysis and complexity bounds that improve state of the art by adapting to two heterogeneity measures, across-group δ1 and within-group δ2.
watch_item: Performance depends on the nested data structure and heterogeneity estimates, so benefits may be reduced if the partitioning is poor or the group structure is not meaningful.
paper_title: SILAGE: Memory-Efficient, Full-Gradient-Free Nonconvex Optimization for Nested Finite Sums
